---
title: "R Notebook"
output: html_notebook
---


```{r}
# Load packages
easypackages::libraries(c("readr","readxl","dplyr","tidyr","ggplot2","ggpubr","UpSetR","cowplot","grid","ggplotify","data.table","plotROC","pROC","lme4", "splines","emmeans","tidytext"))

# insert path to folders
directory_path <- "" # CHANGE THIS to path to director enclosing code and data sets
folder_path <- paste0(directory_path, "/Supplemental Tables/") # folder containing downloaded data sets
intermediate_folder_path <- paste0(directory_path, "/Intermediate Files Used in Plots/") # path containing intermediate outputs
folder_path_save <- paste0(directory_path, "/Complete Figures/")# define folder path to save images to
folder_path_code <- paste0(directory_path, "/Functions and General Analysis Code/") # change this to folder containing downloaded functions and code

# Load Supplemental Data
combined_metadata_dist <- read_csv(paste0(folder_path,"Data Table S1.csv"))
all_zs_combined <- read_csv(paste0(folder_path,"Data Table S2.csv"))
all_hits_combined <- read_csv(paste0(folder_path,"Data Table S3.csv"))
Viral_Annotations <- read_csv(paste0(folder_path,"Data Table S4.csv"))

# Load Intermediate data
Z_fam_cor_combined <- read_csv(paste0(intermediate_folder_path,"Viral_Family_Autocorrelation_Relative_to_initial_samples_by_age.csv"))  # Data frame used was generated in the script "Generate Viral Family Correlation Trajectories.R"
adult_Consec_Zs <- read_csv(paste0(intermediate_folder_path,"Adult_Consec_Zs.csv")) # Data frame used was generated in the script "Generate Consecutive Z Score Files.R"
ped_5_Zs <- read_csv(paste0(intermediate_folder_path,"Ped_5_apart_Zs.csv")) # Data frame used was generated in the script "Generate Consecutive Z Score Files.R"
pal1<-c("#F87E2E", "#FFEC33", "#1FAA79", "#14C4F8", "#0077C8", "#9870F5", "#D53EE1", "#FF36B0", "#D72638", "#6E6E6E")
source(paste0(folder_path_code,"Immunoprint functions.R"))
```

# Prepare Data
Initial consecutive Z Scores files were created in "Generate Consecutive Z Score Files.R"
```{r}
adult_Consec_Zs<-adult_Consec_Zs %>% mutate(time_interval=Age_Years_2-Age_Years_1)
# limit to 4-6 years apart, prioritizing closest to 5 years
adult_Consec_Zs<-adult_Consec_Zs  %>% filter(time_interval>=4,time_interval<=6) %>% mutate(dist_from_5=abs(time_interval-5))%>% group_by(donor,id) %>% arrange(dist_from_5) %>% slice_head(n=1) %>% ungroup() # limit to one observation per donor 4-6 years apart
length(unique(adult_Consec_Zs$donor)) # number of donors = 56
consec_adult_for_jaccard<-adult_Consec_Zs %>% filter(Hit_1==1)
rm(adult_Consec_Zs)
```

Relationship between Z Score and stability
```{r,fig.height=7,fig.width=7.24}
Fig_S5_A<-ggplot(consec_adult_for_jaccard) +
  geom_point(aes(x = Z_1, y = Z_2), color = pal1[3], alpha = 0.5) +
  geom_smooth(aes(x = Z_1, y = Z_2), method = "lm", se = FALSE, color = "black") +
  stat_cor(aes(x = Z_1, y = Z_2), method = "pearson", label.x.npc = "left", label.y.npc = "top",size = 3) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(text=element_text(family="Helvetica", size = 7),
        panel.grid = element_blank(),
        axis.title = element_text(size = 7),
        legend.position = "top") +
  xlab("Adult EBS at Baseline") +
  ylab("Adult EBS 4-6\nyears later") 
# Inital Z > x, whether it will be there
summary(glm(Hit_2~Z_1,family = binomial, data=consec_adult_for_jaccard))
pred_probs <- predict(glm(Hit_2~Z_1,family = binomial, data=consec_adult_for_jaccard), type = "response")
roc_obj <- pROC::roc(consec_adult_for_jaccard$Hit_2, pred_probs)
plot(roc_obj)

ggplot(consec_adult_for_jaccard, aes(d = Hit_2, m = pred_probs)) + 
    geom_roc()+coord_equal()+xlab("Specificity")+ylab("Sensitivity")
ggplot(consec_adult_for_jaccard, aes(d = Hit_2, m = Z_1)) + 
    geom_roc()+coord_equal()+xlab("Specificity")+ylab("Sensitivity")+ theme(panel.grid = element_blank(),axis.title = element_text(size=7),legend.position = "top")

roc_obj2<-pROC::roc(consec_adult_for_jaccard$Hit_2, consec_adult_for_jaccard$Z_1)

coords(roc_obj2, "local maximas", ret = c("threshold", "sensitivity", "specificity","accuracy")) # 6.556995 maximizes accuracy

model <- glm(Hit_2 ~ Z_1, family = binomial, data = consec_adult_for_jaccard)

# Create new data for predictions: generate 100 values of Z_1 spanning its observed range
newdata <- data.frame(Z_1 = seq(min(consec_adult_for_jaccard$Z_1),
                                  max(consec_adult_for_jaccard$Z_1),
                                  length.out = 100))

# Get predicted probabilities from the model
newdata$predicted_prob <- predict(model, newdata = newdata, type = "response")

# Plot with ggplot2
Fig_S5_B<-ggplot() +
  # Plot the predicted probability curve
  geom_line(data = newdata, aes(x = Z_1, y = predicted_prob), color = pal1[6], size = 1.5) +
  # +
  labs(x = "EBS at Baseline", y = "Probability of being\ntargeted 4-6 years later") +
  theme_minimal()+ theme(text=element_text(family="Helvetica", size = 7),panel.grid = element_blank(),axis.title = element_text(size=7),panel.border = element_rect(color = "black",fill = NA),panel.background = element_blank())+scale_x_continuous(trans = "log2",
                     breaks = c(2, 4, 8, 16, 32, 64, 128, 1024),
                     labels = c("2", "4", "8", "16", "32", "64", "128", "1024"))
Fig_S5_B
```

# Figure Ex Dat 2C
# Prepare data for Figure Ex Dat 2C
Jaccard distances for each age group
For each age group, only compute for peptides targeted by at least 5 donors and 20% of the group at baseline
```{r}
# Adult
temp<-consec_adult_for_jaccard %>% group_by(id) %>% summarise(n_donors_targeting=n()) %>% mutate(prop_targeting=n_donors_targeting/56) %>% ungroup() %>% filter(n_donors_targeting>=5,prop_targeting>=0.2) #  1353 peptides meet criteria
jaccard_1_5<-consec_adult_for_jaccard %>% filter(id %in% temp$id)%>% group_by(id) %>% summarise(hit_jaccard=jaccard::jaccard(Hit_1,Hit_2)) %>% left_join(Viral_Annotations %>% select(c(id,species,`Protein names`,public_epitope_2015,family_label)))

# Four year olds
four_Consec_Zs<-ped_5_Zs %>% mutate(time_interval=abs(Age_Years_2-Age_Years_1)) %>% ungroup%>% filter(Age_Years_1>3, Age_Years_1<5, time_interval>=4,time_interval<=6) %>% mutate(dist_from_4=abs(Age_Years_1-4), dist_from_5=abs(time_interval-5)) %>% group_by(donor,id) %>% arrange(dist_from_4, dist_from_5) %>% slice_head(n=1) %>% ungroup() %>% select(-c(dist_from_4,dist_from_5)) # limit to one observation per donor 4-6 years apart
consec_four_for_jaccard <- four_Consec_Zs %>% filter(Hit_1==1)
temp<-consec_four_for_jaccard %>% group_by(id) %>% summarise(n_donors_targeting=n())  %>% mutate(prop_targeting=n_donors_targeting/17)%>% ungroup() %>% filter(n_donors_targeting>=5, prop_targeting>=0.2) # 1846 peptides meet criteria
jaccard_1_5_four<-consec_four_for_jaccard %>% filter(id %in% temp$id)%>% group_by(id) %>% summarise(hit_jaccard=jaccard::jaccard(Hit_1,Hit_2)) %>% left_join(Viral_Annotations %>% select(c(id,species,`Protein names`,public_epitope_2015,family_label)))
rm(four_Consec_Zs)

# One year olds
one_Consec_Zs<-ped_5_Zs %>% mutate(time_interval=abs(Age_Years_2-Age_Years_1)) %>% ungroup%>% filter(Age_Years_1>=0.8,Age_Years_1<=1.5, time_interval>=4,time_interval<=6) %>% mutate(dist_from_1=abs(Age_Years_1-1), dist_from_5=abs(time_interval-5)) %>% group_by(donor,id) %>% arrange(dist_from_1, dist_from_5) %>% slice_head(n=1) %>% ungroup() %>% select(-c(dist_from_1,dist_from_5)) # limit to one observation per donor 4-6 years apart
consec_one_for_jaccard <- one_Consec_Zs %>% filter(Hit_1==1)
temp<-consec_one_for_jaccard %>% group_by(id) %>% summarise(n_donors_targeting=n())  %>% mutate(prop_targeting=n_donors_targeting/14) %>% ungroup() %>% filter(n_donors_targeting>=5,prop_targeting>=0.2) # 1051 peptides meet criteria
jaccard_1_5_one<-consec_one_for_jaccard %>% filter(id %in% temp$id)%>% group_by(id) %>% summarise(hit_jaccard=jaccard::jaccard(Hit_1,Hit_2)) %>% left_join(Viral_Annotations %>% select(c(id,species,`Protein names`,public_epitope_2015,family_label)))
rm(one_Consec_Zs)

# Combine
combined_jaccard <- jaccard_1_5_one %>% mutate(facet_label="One year olds") %>% bind_rows(jaccard_1_5_four %>% mutate(facet_label="Four year olds"))%>% bind_rows(jaccard_1_5 %>% mutate(facet_label="Adults"))
head(combined_jaccard)
```

# Panel
```{r}
s5c_dat <- combined_jaccard %>%
  filter(facet_label != "Four year olds") %>%
  mutate(
    facet_label = ifelse(facet_label == "One year olds", "One-year-olds", facet_label),
    label_pub = ifelse(public_epitope_2015 == TRUE, "Public", "Private")
  )

# Exact two-sided Wilcoxon rank-sum P values for each facet
s5c_pvals <- s5c_dat %>%
  group_by(facet_label) %>%
  summarise(
    p_value = wilcox.test(
      hit_jaccard ~ label_pub,
      data = cur_data(),
      alternative = "two.sided",
      exact = FALSE
    )$p.value,
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    p_label = ifelse(
      p_value < 2.2e-16,
      "P < 2.2e-16",
      paste0("P = ", signif(p_value, 3))
    )
  )

s5c_pvals
Fig_S5_C <- ggplot(
  s5c_dat,
  aes(x = label_pub, y = hit_jaccard)
) +
  geom_violin(alpha = 0.9, aes(fill = facet_label)) +
  geom_signif(
    data = s5c_pvals,
    aes(
      xmin = "Public",
      xmax = "Private",
      annotations = p_label,
      y_position = 1.05
    ),
    manual = TRUE,
    inherit.aes = FALSE,
    textsize = 2.2,
    tip_length = 0.01
  ) +
  ylab("Proportion of individuals\ntargeting peptide 4-6 years later") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    text = element_text(family = "Helvetica", size = 7),
    axis.title.x = element_blank(),
    axis.title.y = element_text(size = 7),
    axis.text = element_text(size = 7),
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45),
    strip.text = element_text(size = 7),
    panel.grid = element_blank(),
    legend.position = "none"
  ) +
  facet_wrap(~facet_label) +
  scale_fill_manual(name = "", values = c(pal1[c(5, 8)])) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 1.1), breaks = c(0, 0.5, 1))

Fig_S5_C
```


# Figure S5 Combined
```{r,fig.width=7,fig.height=2.5}
ggarrange(Fig_S5_A,Fig_S5_B,Fig_S5_C, nrow = 1,widths = c(1,1,1),labels=c("a","b","c"),font.label = list(family="Helvetica",size=8))
ggsave(file=paste0(folder_path_save, "/AVARS_Figure_S5_Final.pdf"))
dev.off
```
